title: Do visualization tools help an LLM agent solve Putnam problems?
description: A/B and ablation study of whether graphical/plotting MCP tools (math-viz) help Claude Code solve Putnam real-analysis problems formalized in Lean 4. Reasoning reconstructed from the graphical-reasoning experiment repo.

glossary.discovery-value decomposition: The framing contribution: end-task tool effect = tool_value x P(adopted). Separates whether the agent USES a tool from whether the tool HELPS; a zero adoption term makes value unmeasurable.
glossary.forced pause: A prompt-mandated commitment to a verdict before answering. Across the studies this required verification step - not the visual modality - is what does the corrective work.
glossary.modality: Which activity satisfies a forced verification: forced_visual (plot + Read), forced_textual (SymPy numeric sample points), forced_reflection (one prose paragraph, no tools). Finding: the modalities are interchangeable.
glossary.path-not-content friction: math-viz originally returned only a PNG filesystem path; the agent never self-initiates the Read to consume it. Generalizes to any tool returning a handle instead of inline content.
glossary.confirmatory vs corrective: Confirmatory = a plot ratifies an answer the model already had (true claims); corrective = a plot overturns a false stated claim (the pilot's decisive use). The program engineered conditions to elicit the corrective mode.
glossary.CoT-incompetence baseline: A corpus where prose chain-of-thought already fails. Required for any tool's value to be detectable; the study corpora had a ~100% CoT baseline (no headroom).
glossary.solved*: The success criterion: build_ok AND zero sorries AND LLM judge verdict in {match, close}.
glossary.picture-decisive and sampling-fragile: Study 8 corpus design criteria: a single plot makes the truth obvious, while a naive uniform numeric grid misses the falsifier (narrow dip, tangency, high-frequency wiggle).
glossary.math-viz: The MCP visualization server under test (14 tools: plots, regions, phase portraits, LaTeX render, ...). Writes a PNG and originally returned its path; the treatment whose adoption the program measures.
